mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand expansion that sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity repricing and generate negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns during tightening periods, with a hit rate below 40 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises above 200.0, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.0.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic capacity and rising wage bills that support corporate revenue growth, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS momentum drops below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or fails to predict positive 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of falling real GDP, weak retail sales momentum triggers immediate liquidity sell-offs that produce negative weekly S&P 500 returns as investors de-risk portfolios.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day SPX returns occur in less than 35 percent of weeks following concurrent declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS during contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling inflation indicates improving supply chain efficiency that boosts profit margins and generates positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling CPIAUCSL yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over a 4-year out-of-sample test.
